executiveSummary.text: STERIS plc (STE) is now back in sync with the canonical DYOR HQ sheet after the metadata repair pass. The current stance is REDUCE with conviction 50/100, using $222.58 as the latest stored price reference where available.

businessModel.text: STERIS plc operates in healthcare equipment and services, with the thesis shaped by recurring demand, procedure volumes, execution and pricing discipline.

recentCatalysts.text: Procedure trends, installed-base growth, service mix and margin delivery remain the main watch points.

thesisEvaluation.text: Base case: the core operating backdrop remains good enough to support the present reduce stance, but not strong enough to justify a looser risk view without better evidence. Bull case: execution and demand improve faster than expected. Bear case: operating or funding pressure worsens and compresses valuation further.

keyRisks.text: Hospital and provider spending can slow. Product execution or recall issues can hurt sentiment. Competition can pressure pricing. M&A or integration steps can disappoint. FX can affect reported growth.

risks.risk: Hospital and provider spending can slow.
Product execution or recall issues can hurt sentiment.
M&A or integration steps can disappoint.

whoShouldOwn.text: Own: Investors wanting healthcare exposure with recurring-service characteristics. Avoid: Investors expecting very rapid growth or no execution risk.

recommendation.text: REDUCE - Conviction: 50/100. This rebuilt page is intentionally concise, but it is now internally coherent and aligned to the corrected ticker and company metadata.

entryExit.text: Keep position sizing consistent with a reduce stance until fuller company-specific analysis is refreshed.
